pi_items.source: task.json specification and setup_task.sh confirms file should be saved with this name
setup_task.sh line 32 creates exactly this path; task.json description references ~/Documents/GeoGebra/assets/coaster_loop.jpg
Standard track gauge for B&M (Bolliger & Mabillard) coasters is 1200mm (1.2 meters). This is widely documented in roller coaster engineering standards.

Coasterpedia states clothoid loops have typical heights of 20-40 meters. The 15m minimum is reasonable as a lower bound for small/compact clothoid loops. Revolution loop is 90 feet (27.43m), well within range.
As of 2025, the tallest vertical loops are 171 feet (52.1 meters) per Coasterpedia. The 60m value is reasonable as a task constraint and exceeds current real-world maxima.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses a real roller coaster (Revolution at Six Flags Magic Mountain) with documented specifications: a clothoid-shaped vertical loop 90 feet tall (27.43m), with 1.2-meter standard track gauge. The image source is Wikimedia Commons (https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/2/29/Revolution_Loop_2.jpg/800px-Revolution_Loop_2.jpg). The Revolution was designed by Werner Stengel and manufactured by Anton Schwarzkopf, opening in 1976 as the world's first modern vertical-looping roller coaster. The task's height range of 15-60 meters aligns with typical clothoid loop specifications (20-40m typical, with current maximum recorded height of 52.1m). The 1.2m track gauge matches B&M coaster standards.
